build_from.reason: Result struct (Findings []Merged, Summary) is the input data for scorecard computation. The Reconcile function produces everything needed: per-reviewer attribution, corroboration counts (via len(group) >= 2), and verification verdicts.
build_from.suggested_approach: Add internal/scorecard/ package that reads reconcile.Result + reconciled/findings.json + reconciled/verification.json, computes per-reviewer metrics, and writes JSONL to ~/.config/atcr/scorecard/YYYY-MM.jsonl. Hook into cmd/atcr/reconcile.go after reconcile.RunReconcile returns successfully.

semantic_matches.relevance: Core pipeline producing Result{Findings, Summary} — primary input for scorecard computation
Merged struct embeds stream.Finding with Reviewers []string — source of per-reviewer attribution and corroboration counts
Verification struct with Verdict enum (confirmed/refuted/unverifiable) — source of survived_skeptic_rate
Hook point for scorecard emission — after reconcile.RunReconcile returns, before gateFindings
Existing JSONL append-only pattern using os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND — reuse for scorecard JSONL storage
chatResponse drops usage field — hard prerequisite for cost_usd/tokens_in/tokens_out columns
Finding struct has Reviewer (single source) and Reviewers ([]string reconciled) — per-reviewer attribution source
fanout.Result carries DurationMS per agent and is the natural seam to carry usage/cost back from the LLM call
Per-agent status.json record carries DurationMS but currently no Model or usage fields; scorecard needs model mapping from registry
AgentConfig binds reviewer name to Model and Role — needed for scorecard reviewer→model mapping
ReviewID derives the run_id for scorecard records
Existing text/tabwriter table pattern — follow for atcr scorecard/leaderboard CLI tables
chatToolResponse also drops usage field — same prerequisite for tool-loop reviewers
Builds AgentStatus from Result; must be extended to include usage/cost and model if scorecard reads status.json
Review directory resolution shared by scorecard/leaderboard commands

existing_patterns.name: Cobra Command Pattern
existing_patterns.description: Each subcommand in its own file under cmd/atcr/ with newXxxCmd() *cobra.Command returning a command with Use, Short, Args (via usageArgs wrapper), RunE. Flags registered via cmd.Flags(). Commands are registered in cmd/atcr/main.go root.AddCommand().

existing_patterns.name: JSONL Append-Only Storage
existing_patterns.description: OpenTranscript uses os.OpenFile with O_CREATE|O_WRONLY|O_APPEND, wraps in bufio.Writer, returns disabled no-op on error. Sticky error via failed bool prevents log flooding. Tests in internal/tools/transcript_test.go cover append-only behavior and file modes.

existing_patterns.name: Reconcile Result Pipeline
existing_patterns.description: reconcile.RunReconcile returns Result{Findings []Merged, Summary}. Merged embeds stream.Finding with Reviewers []string, Verification *Verification. CLI reads via resolveReviewDir then calls RunReconcile. ReadReconciledFindings loads reconciled/findings.json.

existing_patterns.name: Review Directory Resolution
existing_patterns.description: anchorDir/resolveReviewDir in cmd/atcr/anchor.go resolves bare review ID or path to absolute review directory. fanout.EnsureReviewComplete guards against mid-run reconcile.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Scorecard and leaderboard commands need same review-dir resolution

existing_patterns.name: Tabwriter Table Pattern
existing_patterns.description: internal/doctor/render.go uses text/tabwriter.NewWriter with tab-separated header and rows, then Flush. Best practice: return/ignore flush error, use '-' placeholder for missing values.
existing_patterns.follow_for: atcr scorecard and atcr leaderboard table output

existing_patterns.name: Per-Agent Status Record
existing_patterns.description: internal/fanout/status.go defines AgentStatus (agent, status, findings_count, duration_ms, etc.) written to sources/pool/raw/agent/<name>/status.json by internal/fanout/artifacts.go:statusFor. Currently omits Model and usage/cost fields.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Latency_ms source; possible seam for model/usage fields if scorecard reads status.json

existing_patterns.name: Agent Config Model + Role
existing_patterns.description: internal/registry/config.go defines AgentConfig with Provider, Model, Role (reviewer/skeptic/judge), and constraint fields. Registry.Agents maps agent name to config. This is the source of truth for reviewer→model mapping.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Scorecard record model and role fields

integration_points.description: After reconcile.RunReconcile succeeds and before gateFindings, call scorecard.Emit(reviewDir, res, opts) — best-effort, errors logged not returned. Add --no-scorecard flag to skip emission.
Register newScorecardCmd() and newLeaderboardCmd() alongside existing subcommands
Add Usage field to chatResponse; return usage metadata alongside content so callers (engine, doctor) can record tokens_in/tokens_out
Add Usage field to chatToolResponse; return aggregated usage metadata across the multi-turn exchange
Capture usage returned by Complete and store it in Result so status.json can persist tokens/cost per reviewer
Aggregate per-turn Chat usage across tool loop and store it in Result for status.json and scorecard
Optionally extend AgentStatus with Model and usage/cost fields so scorecard can read them directly from status.json; otherwise scorecard must load registry + manifest to map reviewer→model
Record schema + Emit function: compute per-reviewer metrics, resolve reviewer→model via registry or manifest, append JSONL records
Read/query monthly JSONL files under ~/.config/atcr/scorecard/ for scorecard and leaderboard commands
Aggregation logic for leaderboard: rank by corroboration rate, cost-per-corroborated-finding, filters (--since/--model/--persona)
Versioned public submission JSON format (schema v1) with anonymization pass
atcr scorecard [id-or-path] — read reconciled findings + optional status.json and display single-run table
atcr leaderboard [--since, --model, --persona, --export] — aggregate across monthly JSONL and render ranked table

integration_gaps.description: llmclient currently discards provider usage blocks. Before cost_usd/tokens_in/tokens_out can be populated, Complete()/Chat() must decode usage, engine/loop must carry it through Result, and scorecard must receive it. This is the epic's hard prerequisite.
integration_gaps.suggested_fix: Extend chatResponse/chatToolResponse with Usage{PromptTokens, CompletionTokens}; change Complete to return (string, *Usage, error) and Chat to return (*ChatResponse, *Usage, error); thread usage into fanout.Result and AgentStatus. Update all callers: internal/fanout/engine.go, internal/fanout/loop.go, internal/doctor/run.go, and tests.

integration_gaps.description: AgentStatus does not include Model, and findings.json only lists reviewer names. The scorecard record requires model and role. The registry is the source of truth, but the scorecard emitter may not have registry loaded at reconcile time.
integration_gaps.suggested_fix: Option A: extend AgentStatus with Model/Role (written by statusFor from Agent.Invocation.Model and registry Role) so scorecard reads status.json. Option B: scorecard loads ~/.config/atcr/registry.yaml and maps reviewer names to AgentConfig.Model/Role. Option A is cleaner but changes status.json schema.

integration_gaps.description: No per-model pricing table exists in the codebase. cost_usd cannot be computed from token counts without a rate map.
integration_gaps.suggested_fix: Add an internal/scorecard/pricing.go (or internal/llmclient/pricing.go) with a hard-coded or registry-configurable per-model token rate table. Keep it minimal and versioned; omit cost_usd when a model is unknown.

integration_gaps.description: The local store path (~/.config/atcr/scorecard/) is referenced in planning but no shared utility resolves the ATCR config directory.
integration_gaps.suggested_fix: Add internal/scorecard/paths.go (or extend registry/config.go) with ScorecardDir() using os.UserHomeDir + filepath.Join(home, '.config', 'atcr', 'scorecard'), matching registry.DefaultRegistryPath's layout.

integration_gaps.description: The scorecard run_id is described as a timestamp+hash, but reconcile currently derives no such identifier. ReviewID lives in fanout, not reconcile.
integration_gaps.suggested_fix: Use the review directory name (from fanout.ReviewID or the resolved reviewDir base) plus Summary.ReconciledAt as run_id, or generate a deterministic run_id in scorecard.Emit from reviewDir + ReconciledAt. Document the chosen format in docs/scorecard.md.

integration_gaps.description: Requirements say one aggregate record per run is appended in addition to per-reviewer records. The aggregate fields (e.g., total_findings, total_reviewers) are not fully specified.
integration_gaps.suggested_fix: Define an aggregate row with reviewer='__aggregate__' or a separate AggregateRecord struct, including totals and averages. Keep schema_version consistent.

integration_gaps.description: The export format must strip PII, repo content, and provider keys, but the exact allow/deny list is not codified.
integration_gaps.suggested_fix: In export.go, implement an anonymizeRecord function that copies only schema_version, run_id, reviewer hash/anonymous_id, model, role, and numeric metrics; drops file/line/problem/evidence, provider names, and any path strings.

reusable_components.name: resolveReviewDir
reusable_components.usage_example: Shared by reconcile, report, verify — scorecard and leaderboard commands reuse for [id-or-path] arg resolution

reusable_components.name: ReadReconciledFindings
reusable_components.usage_example: Loader for reconciled/findings.json — scorecard uses to count per-reviewer findings and verification status

reusable_components.name: ReadVerificationResults
reusable_components.usage_example: Loader for reconciled/verification.json — scorecard uses when computing findings_verified/refuted/survived_skeptic_rate

reusable_components.name: OpenTranscript pattern
reusable_components.usage_example: os.OpenFile with O_APPEND, bufio.Writer, disabled no-op on error — replicate for scorecard JSONL writer

reusable_components.name: AgentConfig.Model/Role
reusable_components.usage_example: Map reviewer name to model and role by loading registry and looking up Agents[name]

reusable_components.name: RenderTable
reusable_components.usage_example: text/tabwriter table with tab-separated columns and Flush — follow for scorecard/leaderboard CLI output

architecture_notes: Scorecard emission should be best-effort (log-and-continue on error), not a hard failure — reconcile must succeed even if scorecard write fails
The llmclient usage parsing is a hard prerequisite for cost/token columns; without it those columns are always empty. Track as the first task.
Per-reviewer metrics derive from reconciled/findings.json Reviewers []string — count findings where reviewer appears, count where len(Reviewers) >= 2 for corroboration
verification.json presence is optional; when absent, omit findings_verified/findings_refuted/survived_skeptic_rate from the record (omitempty)
Monthly JSONL rotation: file per calendar month (YYYY-MM.jsonl) under ~/.config/atcr/scorecard/
Use text/tabwriter (not markdown) for scorecard/leaderboard tables to align numeric columns
Consider extending AgentStatus with Model/Role/Usage so scorecard can read a single status.json per agent instead of re-loading registry
Keep the scorecard package decoupled from the fan-out engine: it consumes reconcile.Result + reviewDir artifacts, not engine internals
